cover.title: 5 levers separate gains from rework
cover.sub: The research is clear AI underdelivers by default. It's just as clear about why. None of these is a better model.

slides.title: point AI at the right work
slides.body: Stanford measured 35-40% gains on clean code and single digits on complex legacy. Where you aim it decides most of the result.

slides.title: keep batches small
slides.body: AI makes big change sets easy, and DORA tied bigger batches to a 7.2% stability drop. Make review mean something again.

slides.title: measure outcomes, not output
slides.body: Track cycle time, change-failure rate, rework. Lines shipped and percent-AI-code tell you nothing true.

slides.title: serve the agent trusted context
slides.body: Stale context is a root cause under METR and ETH Zurich's findings. Give the agent the currently-correct doc, not a stale file.

slides.title: invest in codebase cleanliness
slides.body: Net AI productivity rose with tests, types, docs, modularity. The lever leaders underrate because it looks like maintenance.

cta.title: score your team on all five
cta.sub: A straight read of your agent setup in your real repo. We map which of the five levers is costing you most, plan yours to keep.
